Choosing between digital certificates and badges is one of the most important decisions before introducing digital credentials into your organisation. Both digital certificates and digital badges have their benefits and uses, which is why this question is relevant: digital badges vs digital certificates: which one should you choose? Whether your organisation is a corporate entity, an educational institution or an NGO, there will always be an opportunity to benefit from introducing digital credentials. For example, they can be used to make your credentials more secure, increase the certification process’s efficiency, or increase brand presence online. However, choosing the right type of digital credential is necessary to achieve these benefits. To help you make this choice, this guide will help you learn what digital certificates and badges are, where they are used and what unique benefits they can bring to your organisation. To get started, let us take a look at what digital certificates and badges are.

sections.heading: What Is A Digital Certificate?
sections.content: A digital certificate is a verifiable document in digital format that proves an individual’s skills, knowledge, experience or achievements. They usually take the form of educational certificates, certificates of achievement, compliance certificates, etc. Digital certificates can be either a portable document (PDF), an image (JPEG, PNG, etc) or a webpage (HTML). Furthermore, they can be encrypted to prevent unauthorised tampering. Digital certificates, like their paper counterparts, describe the skill, knowledge, or achievement they represent, including details about the recipient and the issuer. This is what a digital certificate looks like:

sections.heading: What Is A Digital Badge?
sections.content: On the other hand, a digital badge is a visual representation of an individual’s achievement, skill or accomplishment. It comes with a unique credential ID that can be used to verify its authenticity and can be embedded within a document or webpage. Digital badges almost always consist of an image, with only the name of the skill or achievement it represents. The majority of the information about the digital badge is within the image’s metadata. Due to their simplicity and format, digital badges are often pinned to individuals’ online profiles and provide viewers with information about the recipient’s skill or achievement at a glance. Here’s what they look like. As can be seen from their definitions, digital certificates and badges both represent an individual’s skills and achievements. That’s not the only similarity between them. Before we look into how different digital certificates and badges are, let us first examine how similar they are.

sections.heading: Characteristics Of Digital Certificates And Badges
sections.content: Both digital badges and certificates are digital credentials. This means that they exist in the digital world and not physically. This leads to many characteristics that are shared between them. Here are a few examples:

sections.heading: 1. Enhanced Security
sections.content: Both digital badges and certificates are more secure than their physical counterparts. They use encryption and cryptographic techniques, such as blockchain, to secure the information they contain, making them immune to forgery, theft, fraud, or misrepresentation.

sections.heading: 2. Portability And Accessibility
sections.content: Digital certificates and badges can be stored electronically in storage devices, smartphones, computers, and cloud services. They can also be stored in digital wallets, online profiles, and dedicated credentialing applications. This allows individuals to access them anywhere in the world as long as they have an internet connection.

sections.heading: 3. Shareability
sections.content: Sharing a digital certificate or badge is just as easy as accessing it. They can be shared electronically through emails, social media, instant messaging, or other electronic means, making it easy for individuals to share their credentials with potential employers and other organisations.

sections.heading: 4. Easy Issuance
sections.content: Digital certificates and badges are easier to issue than their physical counterparts. Unlike physical credentials, they can be issued with the click of a button within a credentialing platform, avoiding the need to print and deliver the credentials.

sections.heading: 5. Instant Verification
sections.content: Digital badges and certificates, especially ones anchored on the blockchain, support instant verification. This eliminates the need for an individual or organisation to communicate with the credential issuer to verify its authenticity.

sections.heading: 6. Granular Recognition
sections.content: Both digital certificates and badges support the granular recognition of skills and achievements. Because they are easy to issue and update, individuals can use them to showcase specific skills and competencies, leading to what is known as micro-credentialing. Despite these similarities, digital certificates and badges have certain differences that arise due to their unique characteristics and how they are used. These differences determine which credential is a better fit to fulfil your organisation’s needs.

sections.heading: Digital Certificates vs Digital Badges: Unique Characteristics
sections.content: The unique characteristics of digital certificates and badges are one factor that determines how they are used. Compare their unique characteristics to see how they differ from each other.

sections.heading: 1. Level Of Information
sections.content: Digital certificates are highly informational. They contain information about the course, skill or achievement represented by the certificate, a QR code to verify the certificate’s authenticity and information about both the recipient and the issuer.

sections.heading: 2. Display Of Information
sections.content: The necessary information about the certificate will be displayed within the digital certificate. Thus, viewers can ascertain all the details about the certificate just by viewing it.

sections.heading: 3. Format
sections.content: Digital certificates are electronic versions of paper certificates, which can be portable documents, images, or web pages. Standard formats of digital certificates include PDF, JPEG, PNG, and HTML.

sections.heading: 4. Shareability
sections.content: Digital certificates can be shared as electronic files through email, instant messaging, or any other online communication method. If the certificate is in the form of a webpage, the URL can be shared in the same way. Since digital certificates contain detailed information about the certificate, they can be printed and shared as physical documents.

sections.heading: 1. Level Of Information
sections.content: Due to the format of digital badges, detailed information about the skill or achievement they represent cannot be included. Therefore, only minimal information is provided along with a digital badge.

sections.heading: 2. Display Of Information
sections.content: Since digital badges are relatively small, only the most basic information about the skill or achievement it represents is displayed on the badge; any other necessary information is added to the image’s metadata. The most common method of doing this is by using the OpenBadge format.1

sections.heading: 3. Format
sections.content: Digital badges emulate physical badges. They can be any shape or colour, with unique designs to make them attractive. Digital badges usually come as an image file in JPEG or PNG format.

sections.heading: 4. Shareability
sections.content: Digital badges are designed to be shared and displayed. They have a unique link that can be used to embed them onto online profiles on social media or job-seeking websites or shared directly through email, social media, or instant messaging. As they take the form of an image, they can also be added to documents like resumes. These unique characteristics can influence how digital certificates and badges can be used. Besides their unique characteristics, their application is the most significant factor determining which one is suitable for a particular occasion.

sections.heading: Digital Certificates vs Digital Badges: How They Are Used
sections.content: Digital certificates represent a formal association between an issuer and the recipient, while a digital badge visually represents an individual’s skills and achievements. This and the unique characteristics of digital certificates and badges determine how they’re used. Let’s take a look at how:

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.content: Digital certificates usually represent formal education or official association between two parties. They are universally recognised and can be used to pursue higher education or employment opportunities.

sections.heading: Scope
sections.content: Digital certificates are universally recognised and display all the necessary information, so they have a wider scope of use. They can be used in many different environments and for many different purposes. Since they can be printed, they can even be used in a strictly physical environment.

sections.heading: Who Issues Digital Certificates?
sections.content: Any organisation can issue digital certificates. The common issuers of digital certificates include: Educational institutions E-learning platforms Corporate organisations Training providers Non-governmental organisations

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.content: Digital badges are associated with a visual representation of a skill, achievement or competency. They are awarded for learning a specific skill or completing a specific achievement.

sections.heading: Scope
sections.content: Since digital badges are designed to be displayed in online profiles and documents, and due to the limited amount of display information, their scope of use is very limited.

sections.heading: Who Issues Digital Badges?
sections.content: Just like digital certificates, any organisation can issue digital badges. However, due to their limited scope, digital badges are mostly issued by corporate organisations. Educational institutions and e-learning platforms can still issue them for micro-credentialing and by training providers to showcase competencies. As can be seen from the differences between digital certificates and badges, both have their uses and advantages. These factors determine which credential is best suited for your organisation.

sections.heading: Digital Certificates vs Digital Badges: Which One To Choose?
sections.content: Choosing the type of digital credential is a major step in the credentialing journey. This choice can not only determine the benefits gained from this step but also impact the scope of opportunities available to your organisation in the future. That’s why the choice of type of digital credential is very important. This decision is affected by the following factors:

sections.heading: 1. The Type Of Organisation
sections.content: One of the biggest factors influencing the type of digital credential is the type of organisation issuing it. Organisations representing formal education, such as universities and e-learning platforms, generally issue digital certificates. Comparatively, corporate organisations usually issue digital badges to employees or NGOs to their members.

sections.heading: 2. The Objective Of The Credential
sections.content: Another factor that can determine which type your organisation should choose is the objective of the credential. A digital certificate will be the best choice if the aim is to be a credential that can be used in various situations. If the objective is narrow, such as spreading awareness through social media, digital badges will suffice.

sections.heading: 3. The Goals Of The Recipient
sections.content: When your organisation chooses the type of digital credential, the goals of the credential recipient should also be factored in. If the recipient wants to use the credential in formal settings, such as educational or career development, they prefer to use a digital certificate. However, if their goal is to showcase their skills and achievements on a public platform such as online profiles, then a digital badge is what they will be looking for.

sections.heading: Digital Certificates vs Digital Badges: Why Not Both?
sections.content: Digital certificates and badges may have unique uses. However, your organisation should be open to choice. Credentials issued through CredSure can take the form of both digital badges and digital credentials. If your organisation wants a complete digital credentialing experience, book a demo with CredSure today!

Finding the right credentialing solution for your organisation takes work. Many considerations, such as your overall objectives, budget, and additional benefits, go into selecting a digital credentialing platform. However, some features are fundamental to a rewarding credentialing experience regardless of these factors. To help you choose the perfect digital credentialing solution for your organisation, we’ve compiled a list of 10 necessary features you should look out for. These features will determine whether your credentialing solution is a gimmick or a tool to elevate your credentialing experience to new heights.

sections.heading: 1. Custom Branding
sections.content: Your organisation’s digital credentials are part of your brand identity. They represent the opportunity provided by your organisation to receive this credential. When they are displayed or shared online, your brand will be visible to the outside world. That is why the digital credentialing platform chosen by your organisation must provide the option to include your brand elements in the credential. When you are selecting a digital credentialing solution, ensure that it allows the inclusion of the following: Brand elements such as logos, colours and fonts within the credential Brand elements in the dedicated public verifier page of the credential Customisable email templates for credential issuance, including the sender’s address Customisable URL of the dedicated public verifier page of the credential, with the option to select the domain ID.

sections.heading: 2. Instant Verification
sections.content: One advantage of choosing a digital credentialing solution to issue credentials is the instant verification feature. Verifying a credential usually involves sending a request to the issuer and having them confirm the information with their database. However, that’s not the case with modern digital credentials. Modern digital credentials usually spread their information through cryptographically secured blocks on the internet. This is called anchoring on the blockchain. When a request for verification is made, the information in the credential is verified against the one in the blockchain, a process that takes only a few seconds. This instant verification process can make your organisation’s digital credentials very attractive. And if your digital credentials are attractive, so would your organisation’s learning programs.

sections.heading: 3. Dynamic Formatting
sections.content: Digital credentials come in two forms: digital certificates and digital badges. Each has its different uses, but they can also be used interchangeably in many cases. A single credential can be shared as a digital certificate through your email and then displayed as a digital badge on your social media profile. Your credentialing solution should ideally allow you to issue each credential in either format or both. This should give your recipients more options for displaying and sharing their credentials.

sections.heading: 4. Blockchain Anchoring
sections.content: When the concept of digital credentials was first introduced, they were just digital copies of existing documents, making them prone to tampering. Now that technology has advanced, a better way to secure digital credentials has been found: blockchain anchoring. By anchoring information to the blockchain, a digital credential becomes entirely immune to tampering and permanent. It also assists in instant verification without seeking explicit permission from the issuer. A digital credential without these three characteristics is prone to tampering and loss, less secure and technologically disadvantaged.

sections.heading: 5. Enterprise Data Security
sections.content: Securing the information inside a digital credential is just as important as securing the information of the recipients involved. Any credentialing solution capable of issuing a digital credential will have the option to create a database of recipients with their personal information. Securing this personal information is necessary to protect your recipients against dangerous elements on the internet. Robust security features, including the use of SOC-certified data centres, compliance with GDPR guidelines and enterprise-level security monitoring, are the bare minimum requirements you should expect. Non-compliance with these security standards should be considered a red flag and avoided at all costs. Always read the privacy policy of the credentialing solution of your choice to ensure compliance.

sections.heading: 6. One-Click Social Sharing
sections.content: Sharing a digital credential on social media platforms, especially LinkedIn, has many advantages. For the recipients, it enables them to showcase their skills and achievements to the public and seek opportunities to develop their professional careers. For the issuer, it allows them to expand their brand visibility and improve their brand perception. This is why you should only choose a credentialing solution that allows one-click sharing on social media. This feature not only simplifies sharing a digital credential on social media but also promotes such action using a prominent call to action. Additionally, if a digital credential is shared using this feature, the digital credentialing platform can track the number of credentials shared online. Your organisation can use this information to enhance your marketing strategy.

sections.heading: 7. Easy-To-Use Credential Design
sections.content: Your organisation’s digital credentials represent your brand. Additionally, a visually appealing digital credential will motivate your recipients to display it on social media and share it online. Having easy-to-use design tools built into the digital credentialing platform will enable your organisation to design attractive digital credentials that align with your branding. This feature allows your organisation to differentiate your digital credentials easily, especially when your organisation issues digital credentials for multiple courses.

sections.heading: 8. Analytics And Statistics
sections.content: Tracking your digital credentials goes beyond knowing the number of credentials issued by your organisation. The actions taken by your recipients after receiving their credentials, how often they are shared on social media, verified by employers, etc, are also important indicators of the effectiveness of your credentialing strategy. These indicators can help identify necessary information, including: The effectiveness of your organisation’s marketing strategy If your credentials are shared to prospective employers and if they verify its authenticity If issues are preventing your recipients from sharing your digital credentials on social media The overall success of your credentialing programs

sections.heading: 9. Automated Amendments
sections.content: Once issued, the control of a digital credential remains with the issuer. This means your organisation can make any additional changes to the digital credential. Unlike traditional paper credentials, however, these amendments can automatically update the digital credentials. This works because the digital credential is anchored on the blockchain. Once any change is made, the information on the blockchain is updated, automatically updating the digital credential. With this feature, your organisation retains complete control of any digital credential issued. It also simplifies the process of amending any issued credential.

sections.heading: 10. LMS Integration
sections.content: The easiest way to issue digital credentials is by integrating your chosen credentialing solution with a Learning Management System. With this integration, digital credentials can be issued automatically when a learner completes a program in the LMS. This feature will enable your organisation to fully automate the issue of digital credentials, saving your organisation enormous amounts of time and effort. One additional benefit of this feature is how it simplifies the process of introducing gamification into your organisation’s learning programs. This will not only increase the rate of engagement but also motivate your learners to learn more.

sections.heading: CredSure: The Perfect Digital Credentialing Solution
sections.content: Selecting the right credentialing solution for your organisation is a time-consuming process. These features will give you a reference on what to look for when you choose a digital credentialing platform for your organisation. CredSure offers all the above features and more. Artificial intelligence has rapidly transformed various sectors, and education is no exception. Integrating artificial intelligence in education offers an abundance of opportunities. It can enhance learning opportunities, personalise the learning experience and streamline the administrative process. Education stands as a pillar of societal progress, moulding the minds of future generations and sparking innovation. Historically, educational methods have been one-size-fits-all, lacking the flexibility to cater to the unique needs of each student. Yet, with the rise of AI, this outdated approach is being transformed. AI is revolutionising education by personalising learning experiences, making education more inclusive, dynamic, and effective than ever before. The introduction of artificial intelligence in education has brought about opportunities for a more personalised, efficient, and effective learning experience. This blog explores the transformative impact of artificial intelligence in education, the ethical challenges involved, and how it will change the future.

sections.heading: How Is Artificial Intelligence Used In Education
sections.content: The impact of AI in education can be seen in three major areas: personalised learning, enhanced teaching methods, and administrative efficiency. Let’s examine how artificial intelligence influences these three.

sections.heading: I. Personalised Learning
sections.content: One of the most significant impacts of artificial intelligence in education is enabling personalised learning. Traditional classroom settings often struggle to cater to individual students’ varying learning paces and styles. AI addresses this by providing tailored learning experiences that adapt to each student’s unique needs. AI helps to provide personalised learning experiences in two ways:

sections.heading: 1. Adaptive Learning Platforms
sections.content: Adaptive Learning Platforms use AI algorithms to analyse students’ learning patterns and adjust the content accordingly. These platforms can identify areas where students struggle and provide additional resources or alternative explanations.

sections.heading: 2. Intelligent Tutoring Systems
sections.content: Intelligent Tutoring Systems combine the power of Adaptive Learning Platforms and generative AI to provide one-to-one tutoring that mimics human tutors. These systems use AI to understand students’ strengths and weaknesses and provide personalised feedback and guidance.

sections.heading: II. Enhanced Teaching Methods
sections.content: Just like AI can help students learn more efficiently, it can also help teachers teach. By leveraging AI tools, educators can enhance their teaching methods, making them more effective and engaging. In fact, 52% of teachers already claim to be using AI to enhance their teaching methodology.1 Some of the ways artificial intelligence can enhance teaching methods are as follows:

sections.heading: 1. Automated Grading And Feedback
sections.content: When combined with the power of AI to analyse vast amounts of data objectively, generative AI can not only grade assignments but also provide insightful feedback. This can vastly improve the efficiency of teaching and learning.

sections.heading: 2. Improved Lesson Plans
sections.content: By analysing information on the Internet and the course requirements, AI can create lesson plans tailored to the curriculum. Furthermore, it can efficiently adapt lesson plans to new changes and information.

sections.heading: 3. Content Creation
sections.content: Generative Artificial Intelligence in education can make the curriculum more engaging by creating quizzes, practice questions and reference material. It can even generate images, videos and voice-overs to increase engagement.

sections.heading: 4. AI Assistants
sections.content: AI-based virtual assistants can help manage class schedules, provide real-time answers to student queries, and monitor student participation. This can significantly improve teachers’ efficiency and overall student engagement.

sections.heading: III. Administrative Efficiency
sections.content: The impact of artificial intelligence in education is not limited to learning and teaching. Even the administrative aspect of education can be significantly enhanced by AI, especially through automation. Here are a few ways the administrative efficiency of educational institutions is improved by the impact of AI in education:

sections.heading: 1. Streamlined Admission Process
sections.content: AI can streamline admissions by automating application reviews, scoring standardised tests, and conducting initial interviews through AI-driven chatbots. This ensures a more efficient and fair admissions process.

sections.heading: 2. Predictive Analytics For Student Success
sections.content: AI can use predictive analytics to identify students at risk of failing or dropping out, allowing institutions to intervene early and provide the necessary support. A pilot study by Ivy Tech Community College showed that 98% of students improved their grades after being identified by AI.2

sections.heading: 3. Resource Management
sections.content: AI can optimise resource allocation within educational institutions. For example, AI algorithms can help manage schedules, allocate classroom spaces, and predict future staffing needs. This ensures that resources are used efficiently, reducing operational costs and improving overall effectiveness. The impact of AI in education is immense. In today’s competitive world, educational institutions cannot ignore such benefits by improving the learning, teaching, and administrative aspects of education. However, this does not mean no challenges are involved in adapting artificial intelligence in education.

sections.heading: Ethical Considerations And Challenges
sections.content: The challenges posed by implementing artificial intelligence in education are less of a technical nature and more ethical. Some of the ethical challenges that pose the most significant concern are as follows:

sections.heading: 1. Data Privacy and Security
sections.content: AI systems rely on large amounts of data to function effectively, raising concerns about data privacy and security. Educational institutions must comply with data protection regulations and implement robust security measures to protect student data.

sections.heading: 2. Bias and Fairness
sections.content: AI algorithms can sometimes perpetuate biases in the data they are trained on, leading to unfair treatment of specific student groups. It is crucial to develop and use AI systems that are transparent and fair, ensuring that they do not reinforce existing inequalities.

sections.heading: 3. Accessibility and Equity
sections.content: While AI has the potential to make education more accessible, there is a risk that it could widen the digital divide. Students in underprivileged areas may not have access to the necessary technology or internet connectivity to benefit from AI-driven educational tools. Ensuring equitable access to AI technologies is essential to prevent further educational disparities. These challenges should not be seen as a limitation of implementing artificial intelligence in education but as challenges that must be overcome to create a more robust system. Despite these challenges, the potential of incorporating AI into education guarantees its future.

sections.heading: The Future Of Artificial Intelligence In Education
sections.content: The future of AI in education looks promising, with continued advancements expected to bring even more innovative solutions. Here are some trends and predictions for the future:

sections.heading: Lifelong Learning and Continuous Education
sections.content: AI will play a crucial role in promoting lifelong learning and continuous education. As the job market evolves, individuals must continuously update their skills. AI can provide personalised learning paths and recommendations, making it easier for individuals to stay updated with industry trends and demands.

sections.heading: Global Classroom and Collaborative Learning
sections.content: AI can facilitate global classrooms, where students from different parts of the world can learn together. AI-driven translation tools can break down language barriers, enabling collaborative learning on a global scale.

sections.heading: Emotional AI and Well-being
sections.content: Emotional AI, which can detect and respond to students’ emotional states, has the potential to impact education significantly. By understanding students’ emotions, AI can provide support and interventions to promote mental well-being and create a more conducive learning environment.

sections.heading: AI in Special Education
sections.content: AI holds significant promise for special education, offering tools that can cater to the specific needs of students with disabilities. AI-powered applications can provide personalised learning experiences, helping these students achieve their full potential. Artificial Intelligence is undeniably transforming education, offering unprecedented opportunities to enhance learning experiences, improve teaching methods, and streamline administrative processes. AI is paving the way for a more effective and inclusive educational system by personalising learning, enhancing teaching methods, and increasing administrative efficiency. However, addressing the ethical considerations and challenges associated with its use is crucial to ensure that AI benefits all students equally. As we move forward, embracing artificial intelligence in education will be essential for preparing future generations for the challenges and opportunities of the 21st century. By leveraging the power of AI, we can create a more adaptive, personalised, and equitable educational system that meets the diverse needs of learners worldwide. Are you looking for more ways to improve your learning programs? Book a demo with CredSure and see how digital certificates can enhance your learning programs.

Recent studies have shown that only 8% of consumers trust the words of marketers.1 That leaves 92% who don’t. As the trust in traditional marketing takes a nosedive, how can your organisation convince your customers? The answer is internal marketing. So why internal marketing? Your regular employee is the most trusted voice in your organisation.2 With social media as popular as it is today, your employees are louder than ever, and they are heard by your customers. However, convincing your employees to promote your business is more challenging than paying marketers. For employees to promote your business, they must believe that your organisation is worth the vocal support. The best way to do this is to recognise their contributions and achievements. Digital badges are one way to do this. They can build employee engagement and convince them to promote your brand online, making them ideal tools for internal marketing purposes. Internal marketing is a necessity in the present. This article will explore internal marketing, why you should do it, and how to use digital badges for internal marketing.

sections.heading: What Is Internal Marketing?
sections.content: Internal marketing is promoting your organisation’s vision, values, and objectives to your employees to gain their support. At present, this means convincing them to share positive opinions about your organisation on their personal social media profiles. That’s because around 50% of employees talk about their workplace on social media.3 They are a valuable resource for promoting your business online. Internal marketing is not limited to social media, however. There are as many ways to pursue it as there are traditional ones. Despite that, the core components of internal marketing remain the same.

sections.heading: 1. Employee Engagement
sections.content: The cornerstone of internal marketing is employee engagement. The more engaged your employees are with your organisation, the higher the chance they will post about your business. That is why internal marketing emphasises investing in initiatives that promote employee satisfaction, motivation, and loyalty. These initiatives can include recognition programs, team-building activities, and opportunities for professional growth. This is where digital badges can shine. There are many ways digital badges can enhance employee engagement. It can aid employee training and development, provide recognition, and be shared on social media. Through all these, digital badges can vastly increase employee engagement.

sections.heading: 2. Communication Channels
sections.content: Any form of marketing success relies heavily on the communication channels being used. The same is true for internal marketing. Communication channels are chosen based on the messaging and objectives of the organisation. Internal marketing is unique because of the messaging your organisation needs to promote. If the objective is to promote engagement and enhance loyalty, the communication channels used should reflect that. Transparency is key here. Internal marketing requires an organisational-level communication channel for feedback, suggestions, and information sharing. Regular team meetings, newsletters, and social media groups can also be useful channels for internal marketing.

sections.heading: 3. Professional Development
sections.content: When your employees discuss your organisation on social media, they are more likely to discuss their personal experiences rather than your offerings. One way to ensure that these opinions stay positive is to promote your employees’ professional development. Recognising their achievements, providing rewards, and giving them opportunities to upskill themselves are topics your employees will gladly share online. Again, digital badges can complement this. They are easily shareable on social media, can be used to recognise achievements, and can be rewarded to employees. They are also a great tool to aid the professional development of your employees. It is an understatement to say that internal marketing benefits are limited to your employees promoting your business on social media. When done right, internal marketing can offer even more benefits.

sections.heading: 1. Increased Employee Engagement
sections.content: Through regular communication and involvement in the organisation, employees can feel a higher sense of engagement with your organisation. And, a higher engagement can lead to an increase in profitability up to 23%.4

sections.heading: 2. Enhanced Brand Visibility
sections.content: Your employees talking about your organisation on social media is free marketing. You can increase your brand visibility without paying for it on your employees’ social media networks.

sections.heading: 3. Improved Trust
sections.content: As mentioned at the beginning of this article, your employees are the most trusted voice in your organisation. Your customers will trust their opinions when they speak positively about your organisation.

sections.heading: 4. Higher Customer Engagement
sections.content: Posting on social media is not a monologue. Most platforms offer the ability to reply and post comments, which means that your customers can interact with your employees and engage with your brand.

sections.heading: 5. Boost Brand Perception
sections.content: Customers will see an organisation that takes good care of its employees more positively. After all, if an organisation does not take good care of its employees, how can it expect to take care of its customers? Looking at these benefits, it is easy to see how digital badges can complement internal marketing. From increasing employee engagement to enhancing brand visibility, digital badges are the perfect tool to assist your internal marketing efforts. To help you enhance your internal marketing efforts, let’s now look at how you can use digital badges for internal marketing.

sections.heading: 1. Include Brand Elements
sections.content: Your digital badges should contain your brand elements, such as your brand name, logo, brand colours, etc. They are to remind your employees and the audience that your organisation is responsible for taking steps to empower your employees.

sections.heading: 2. Communicate With Your Stakeholders
sections.content: Communication is a core component of internal marketing. That is why it is necessary to seek suggestions and feedback from your employees about implementing digital badges. This way, they will feel more involved in the process and increase overall engagement.

sections.heading: 3. Create A Culture Of Sharing On Social Media
sections.content: A culture of promoting social media activity can go a long way in convincing your employees to share their achievements online. Provide a “share on social media” button with your digital badges so your employees can easily share them online.

sections.heading: 4. Implement Gamification
sections.content: Gamification can enhance employee engagement by providing an avenue for friendly competition and instant gratification. Implement digital badges to introduce gamification concepts into your professional development programs.

sections.heading: 5. Highlight Achievements
sections.content: Digital badges are a great way to track employee achievements and skills. Your organisation can go further and highlight employees who earn digital badges by sharing their achievements on social media or rewarding them for the number of badges earned. This will promote a culture of active participation in seeking achievements and upskilling opportunities. Employee engagement and overall professional development can be increased this way. The advantages of digital badges go beyond their role in internal marketing and promoting employee advocacy. They are a great tool to enhance employee training and development.

sections.heading: CredSure: Redefining The Future Of Digital Badges
sections.content: CredSure recognises the impact digital badges can have on your organisation. As one of the leading digital badge providers in the world, CredSure’s platform is designed to help organisations use them in unique ways. To promote social sharing, the digital badges created with CredSure’s platform come with a button for easy sharing on social media. CredSure’s platform allows your organisation to customise digital badges by including brand elements. And that’s not all. CredSure’s platform is designed to integrate fully with learning management systems using API. This will let your organisation seamlessly include digital badges in your professional development programs. In conclusion, internal marketing is essential in today’s market. With more customers losing trust in traditional marketing campaigns, internal marketing can be the answer. It can boost employee engagement and encourage them to share positive opinions about your organisation, ultimately improving brand perception. Internal marketing, when coupled with digital badges, can provide many incredible benefits to your organisation. If your organisation wants to enhance its internal marketing efforts with digital badges, book a demo with CredSure today!

In today’s competitive market, succeeding in a business is not entirely dependent on your ability to sell your offerings. With 87% of organisations reporting difficulties finding qualified talent,1 continuing to operate an organisation itself is a challenging issue. The solution for this? Employee training programs. Granted, your organisation can always seek new recruits from the market. However, training employees has more benefits than bringing new skills into your organisation. Benefits that will, in turn, help your organisation grow. What are these benefits? Take a look below:

sections.heading: 1. It Improves Employee Retention
sections.content: Investing in employee training and development programs has been shown to increase employee retention by up to 58%.2 When your organisation implements an employee training program, you are showing your employees that you are ready to invest in their professional development. Employee training and development programs work so well to retain employees due to one major reason. Your employees are always seeking opportunities to grow professionally. In fact, a recent study showed that 86% of employees are ready to move to an organisation that is ready to support their professional development.3

sections.heading: 2. It Increases Productivity
sections.content: A higher productivity means an improved performance of the organisation’s operations. By investing in employee training and development programs, your organisation can see an increase in productivity of up to 24%.4 Increase efficiency, performance, and overall profitability by investing in your employees and giving them an opportunity to grow.

sections.heading: 3. It Attracts Qualified Talents
sections.content: Just like employee training programs can help retain your employees, the promise of opportunities for professional development can attract new recruits to your organisation. Studies have shown that 76% of employees are always on the lookout for opportunities to expand their careers.5 By investing in employee training and development programs, your organisation can give them that opportunity.

sections.heading: 4. It Enhances Employee Engagement
sections.content: Increasing employee engagement can be very beneficial to your organisation. It can not only motivate your employees to perform better but also ensure that they are personally invested in the success of your organisation. But that’s not all. Employee engagement can directly lead to an increase in profitability by up to 23%.5 With so many benefits, implementing employee training and development programs is not a simple task. By definition, an employee training program only needs to provide opportunities for your employees to learn new skills. However, to maximise its benefits, it is necessary to enhance its effectiveness.

sections.heading: 1. Implement Gamification
sections.content: Gamification is one of the most effective ways to enhance employee training programs. Implementing gamification can do wonders for your employee training and development program. Here are a few of the benefits gamification can bring.

sections.heading: Benefits Of Gamification:
sections.content: Your employees will feel 68% more motivated to succeed in the employee training and development programs.6 It can increase learner engagement by 65%.7 The performance of your employees in the corporate training program will increase by 90%.8 There are many ways to implement gamification in your corporate training programs, from instant rewards to ranking lists. One effective way to incorporate gamification into your employee training program is to introduce digital badges. Digital badges are a great tool to enhance gamification. They can be used as a reward for completing a course, for creating rank lists, and for sharing on social media by your employees. They are verifiable proof of achievement, aiding your employees’ professional development.

sections.heading: 2. Use A Learning Management System
sections.content: Learning Management Systems, or LMS, are great tools for employee training and development programs. With an LMS, your organisation can take a back seat and let a complex computer program manage your training program. LMS offers many advantages to your organisation and its employee training programs.

sections.heading: Benefits Of LMS
sections.content: Your organisation can digitise its corporate training programs Automate grading, tracking, and enrollment of training programs Make your employee training and development program available online Integrate AI and digital badges into your employee training program

sections.heading: 3. Introduce Digital Badges
sections.content: Digital badges are generally used in two areas. One is in education, where they act as proof of skill or completion of a course. The other is in organisations where they are rewarded for accomplishments or achievements and for verifying employees’ skills. Therefore, it is no surprise that they are ideally suited for employee training and development programs. A few of the benefits digital badges can bring to your organisation are given below:

sections.heading: Benefits Of Digital Badges
sections.content: It can increase employee engagement and motivation It supports professional development by providing verifiable proof of skill It fosters a community of learning through mutual support and friendly competition It can integrate with LMS and can be used to track the progress of employee training programs It enables sharing on social media platforms It supports gamification initiatives by acting as a reward for achievements Besides training employees, digital badges can also be used in hiring and skills gap assessment. They are a veritable tool that can help your organisation thrive.

sections.heading: 4. Provide Personalised Learning
sections.content: As beneficial as corporate training programs are, not all employees may appreciate the additional requirements made of them. Many constraints like time and effort are required to prevent many employees from pursuing this program. However, by providing personalised learning, your employees can pursue this opportunity to enhance their professional development at their convenience.

sections.heading: 5. Promote Transparency And Communication
sections.content: One of the main goals of providing employee training and development programs is to retain existing employees. By displaying your organisation’s willingness to involve them in the program’s design, your employees will be shown how invaluable they are to your organisation. Communication and transparency will enable your employees to participate proactively in your corporate training programs. They can also help them assess the objectives of this program and take steps to improve it.

sections.heading: 6. Encourage Feedback
sections.content: As one of the major stakeholders of your employee training program, your employees have first-hand experience of how the program is doing. They will be in a position to provide insightful opinions that can improve its effectiveness and tackle issues holding it back. By acting on the feedback given by your employees, they will be shown how much your organisation values their input. This, in turn, will ensure that their loyalty to your organisation remains high.

sections.heading: 7. Support Social Recognition
sections.content: According to Maslow’s hierarchy of needs, all individuals seek social recognition somehow. By enabling your employees to share their progress in employee training and development programs, your organisation can support them in gaining social recognition. This support can take the form of shareable rewards such as digital badges, dedicated social media posts about employees who complete the program, or employee highlights in general. This has the additional benefit of increasing employee engagement and improving your organisation’s brand perception.

sections.heading: 8. Monitor And Improve
sections.content: With tools like LMS and digital badges to enhance employee training programs, your organisation can easily monitor your employees’ progress. However, monitoring is only the first step. By analysing this information, your organisation can identify trends and areas of improvement. By constantly monitoring, analysing, and improving its corporate training programs, your organisation will create an organic, ever-evolving program that will one day become the cornerstone of your organisation’s progress. To summarise, employee training programs are integral to growing your organisation in today’s competitive environment. However, implementing a corporate training program that maximises effectiveness is just as important. With tools like LMS and digital badges, your organisation can enhance its corporate training programs by a vast margin. Do you want to see how digital badges can further enhance employee training programs? Check out this blog.

The role of Artificial Intelligence in training your workforce cannot be overstated. With AI developing at a staggering rate and becoming capable of performing more complex tasks, its impact is shaping how the world works. From boosting efficiency to enhancing performance, the benefits of using AI make it very much suited to the workspace. This is the primary reason why the use of Artificial Intelligence in the workplace is growing rapidly. Research indicates that currently, 32% of organisations employ AI, and a further 42% are contemplating its integration in the near future.1 AI is an invaluable tool for organisations looking for solutions to maximise employee training and development. This article will discuss how AI in training can help your organisation enhance its workforce development programs.

sections.heading: How Will Artificial Intelligence In Training Help Your Organisation?
sections.content: AI, by itself, is a handy tool for any organisation. However, when it comes to workforce development, it can provide a host of unique benefits to your organisation that can vastly enhance its learning programs. These are some of the ways AI can enhance training and development for employees in your organisation:

sections.heading: 1. Provide Personalised Learning
sections.content: When it comes to training a large number of employees, it may seem efficient to provide a holistic training module that works for everyone. After all, creating a personalised training module for each employee would require a team dedicated to the task. The obvious drawback of this type of training is that the employee will have to learn knowledge that they already possess or that is not even required for their role. AI easily solves this. Artificial Intelligence can adapt to the learner’s requirements, automatically crafting a personalised learning programme that is best suited for them. This way, your organisation can ensure that your employees’ time and resources are spent efficiently.

sections.heading: 2. Identify Knowledge Gaps
sections.content: The skills gap is a major concern for any organisation. Up to 87% of employers are estimated to be facing issues finding qualified talent.2 One way to remedy this is through employee training and development. AI helps with this issue by identifying knowledge gaps in your workforce. By providing detailed information about your employees’ skills, AI can identify areas where an infusion of knowledge is required and find employees within the organisation with the right skill set who can use that knowledge effectively. Your organisation can integrate digital badges with your learning management system to make this process even more efficient. By providing digital badges for each skill processed by an employee, your organisation help AI identify potential knowledge gaps.

sections.heading: 3. Creation Of Content
sections.content: Generative AI is one of the latest upgrades to artificial intelligence. It uses natural language processing and other algorithms to generate textual and visual content. This helps create content that enhances the training module for learning and development programs. AI can help create content for your learning programs, from infographics to quizzes. It can also use internet resources to provide snippets and insights about certain topics in a way that your learner can easily consume. This can immensely reduce the cost of creating training modules for your employees, making training and development more efficient for your organisation.

sections.heading: 4. Time Use Efficiency
sections.content: Artificial intelligence is closely connected to computers and the Internet. Today’s AI tools only require these two resources to be accessible. This means that employees can access your training and development programs from anywhere at any time. This way, they can improve their knowledge and skills at their convenience while sitting at home or in transit. A website linked to your learning management system or a specially designed app could make learning and development a convenient and engaging experience.

sections.heading: 5. Instant Assessment And Grading
sections.content: The concept behind AI is a process known as machine learning. By constantly learning about “what is right” and “what is wrong” about a particular topic, the program constantly evolves to become “always right.” This makes AI a great tool for testing the learners’ learning and providing instant assessment. Generative AI can also provide feedback and ways to improve. This drastically reduces the cost of human resources needed for a fully-fledged learning and development program.

sections.heading: 6. Constant Support
sections.content: A learning and development program cannot succeed just by feeding information to the learner. A successful learning program must answer queries and clear doubts when they arise. Once again, the power of AI can be seen here. Using AI-powered chatbots, your organisation’s learning management system can provide instant answers to any doubts and queries posed by your learners. Artificial Intelligence can also help your organisation tag these questions automatically and help you improve your learning programs for the next batch of learners.

sections.heading: 7. Gamification Of Learning
sections.content: AI is adept at collecting information. When coupled with Generative AI, this will allow your learning programs to display real-time rankings and automatically provide rewards. All of these form an integral part of gamification. As studies have shown, gamification can raise the level of learner engagement by up to 65%.3 With artificial intelligence at your fingertips, gamification is easy to implement. Encouraging your employees with digital badges can enhance their learning experience, creating a solid partnership.

sections.heading: 8. Analysis Of Data
sections.content: One of the biggest advantages of Artificial Intelligence in corporate training is its ability to organise and analyse large amounts of information. AI can analyse your learning management system’s information to make crucial insights into your training programs. This will allow your organisation to receive comprehensive reports on the success of your learning programs, identify trends, and identify areas for improvement. These insights can also help your organisation with skills gap assessments, improving productivity.

sections.heading: 9. Training Automation
sections.content: Once an Artificial Intelligence program is set up, it can function autonomously. This means that AI can run training programs without further input when it comes to employee training and development. From analysing data from your learning management systems to generating content, AI can provide all the tools necessary for an employee to upskill themselves. And, if you have set up your learning management system to integrate digital credentials, it can automatically issue them once an employee finishes a course. All in all, using Artificial Intelligence in training your employees is beneficial in many ways. It can improve the efficiency of your learning programs and provide your learners with an engaging learning experience. By further integrating your learning management system with digital badges, you can also ensure that you can maximise the effectiveness of your learning programs. If you want to learn how digital badges can help your organisation, book a demo with CredSure today!

Educational certificates are more than documents proving the educational qualifications of an individual. They are a key that opens the doorway to the professional world and allows one to pursue the career of their choice. Educational certificates are an incredibly useful tool for any professional to advance in their careers. However, as useful as they are, they have their own limitations, for example, a weakness to forgery or the difficulty of verification. However, that is changing with modern technology like Blockchain paving the way. Educational certificates are going through a transformation. No more will they have their previous limitations as they transition to digital certificates. Digital certificates are now the future of educational certification. With new features and added benefits, digital certificates are helping to transform educational certificates into a smarter version of themselves. This article will explore the benefits of digital certificates and why you should adopt them to enhance your learning programs.

sections.heading: What Are Digital Certificates?
sections.content: A digital certificate is a document in an electronic format recognising a skill or achievement. They have a public key that is used to validate the authenticity of the certificate by verifying it against a database. Digital certificates can be shared effortlessly while maintaining their authenticity. They are safer, more convenient and easily accessible. They can be used in different settings. For example, they are used as an alternative to paper certificates issued by educational institutions or as proof of achievement made by an employee in an organisation. How they work can change depending on the setting they are used in. In this article, let’s take a look at how they work for institutions.

sections.heading: Issuance
sections.content: Your institution can issue them in place of traditional educational certificates or as a digital version of the same without being a replacement. This can be done manually by your institution, automated through your LMS or by partnering with a credentialing platform like CredSure.

sections.heading: Receipt
sections.content: Once the digital certificate is issued, the recipient will receive it in their email, along with an offline copy of the certificate if your institution wills it. This copy can either be an image or a document. The actual digital certificate can be accessed through the link provided or by scanning the QR code in the offline copy. This will take the recipient to the public verifier page, where your institution can provide detailed information about the educational certificate.

sections.heading: Display And Sharing
sections.content: Once the recipient has received the digital certificate, it can be shared on social media. For the sake of convenience, CredSure’s digital certificates come with a one-click share option for multiple social media platforms. Your learners can also display them in digital credential wallets or online job profiles like SkillPass, and make them visible to future employers.

sections.heading: Verification
sections.content: When the digital certificates are shared with an employer, they can click the link provided or scan the QR code to visit the public verifier page. Here, they can validate the authenticity of the digital certificate by verifying it against the information anchored on the Blockchain. CredSure’s digital certificates will also enable the viewers to verify the ownership of the digital certificate by validating the phone number/email address of the recipient. This will ensure that the educational certificates issued by your institution will always be authentic and resistant to forgery. Maintaining authenticity is only one of the benefits of educational certificates going digital. There are numerous ways digital educational certificates can benefit your institution and empower your learners. Take a look below for some of these benefits.

sections.heading: Tamper-Proof
sections.content: One of the characteristics of being Blockchain-anchored is immutability. Once an educational certificate is anchored on the Blockchain, it becomes immune to any tampering or alterations. This means that every certificate issued by your institution will be resistant to any fraudulent alterations or misrepresentations.

sections.heading: Instant Verification
sections.content: Another benefit of being Blockchain-anchored is the ease of verification. Unlike traditional paper certificates, which require a lengthy process to verify their authenticity, digital certificates can be verified from any place at any time. Since the information about the certificate is anchored on the blockchain, it becomes possible for anyone to verify the authenticity with a single click.

sections.heading: Global Accessibility
sections.content: Digital certificates, due to their electronic nature, can be accessed from anywhere in the world with an internet connection. They can be stored on any device or on the cloud, viewed from anywhere, and shared through a multitude of platforms, including email, social media, and instant messaging. This accessibility will enable your learners to easily share their credentials with future employers and give them a competitive edge in the hiring process.

sections.heading: Storage And Backup
sections.content: Educational certificates are incredibly important because they represent the completion of a resource-intensive course or program. That’s why it is necessary to store them in a secure location to prevent theft or loss. However, digital certificates do not have this issue. Due to their nature, there is no distinction between an original and a duplicate. They can be duplicated endlessly and stored in multiple devices or locations. As long as one has the link to the certificate, they will have access to it.

sections.heading: Marketability
sections.content: The easily shareable nature of digital certificates has an additional benefit to your institution. They can be used as a marketing tool to increase your brand presence online. When digital certificates are shared online, especially on social media, your institution can increase its visibility and recruit more learners for your learning programs.

sections.heading: Cost Efficiency
sections.content: The final benefit of going the digital route for your educational certificates is the cost of resources. Unlike traditional paper certificates, which use paper, ink, printing and other logistical services, digital certificates are virtually free to issue.

sections.heading: The Future Of Educational Certificates: Smarter And More Secure
sections.content: To tackle the ever-evolving digital landscape, one must adapt to new technologies. As employers look for smarter, more efficient ways to bridge the skills gap, it is the duty of educational institutions to give their learners the right tools to succeed. By adopting digital certificates, your institution is not just making it easy to issue credentials. With digital educational certificates, you empower your learners and help recruiters verify skills more easily. The digital era is an era of transparency, efficiency and authenticity. By choosing to adopt digital certificates, your institution can rise above the competitors. Want to learn how to digitise your educational certificates? Book a demo with us!

Competency-based learning in education is becoming popular day by day. Studies show that up to 82% of educational institutions have already adopted a competency-based learning model1 or are in the process of adopting it. What makes competency-based education so important? From enhancing the learning experience to preparing the learners to tackle the job market, competency-based learning can bring incredible benefits to your institution. This article will take a look at these benefits while exploring what competency-based learning is and why your institution should adopt it.

sections.heading: What Is Competency-Based Learning?
sections.content: Competency-based learning is an educational method that places greater emphasis on skill development over memorisation. The ultimate goal of this model is to help your learners master a particular skill set. This is done through a competency-based assessment of the learner, where they will be tested on their mastery of the skill rather than their knowledge. Competency-based learning and assessment is learner-centric, as opposed to being knowledge-centric like traditional education methods. However, that is not the only difference between competency-based learning and traditional education.

sections.heading: 1. Structure
sections.content: Traditional education takes the form of fixed curriculum courses with a predetermined duration. Failure to complete the course mostly leads to the learner having to repeat the entire course. Competency-based learning and assessment differ from this in that the course can only be completed upon the mastery of a skill. There is no fixed duration for the course, allowing the learners to complete the curriculum at their own pace.

sections.heading: 2. Learning Outcome
sections.content: A competency-based learning model places emphasis on skill development. Therefore, the learning outcome is based on the mastery of a skill and its practical application. This is evaluated using competency-based assessment tests. This is different from traditional education, where the learning outcome is centred on the comprehension of the subject material and evaluated using end-of-the-term examinations.

sections.heading: 3. Grading
sections.content: In traditional education models, the learners are graded based on their test scores, assignments and behaviour. This is opposed to competency-based learning models, where the learners are graded based on their competency in the practical application of a skill. These differences do not mean that competency-based learning is better than traditional education or that it is a replacement for it. It just means that competency-based learning is suited to specific applications and brings unique benefits to the learners. These benefits enable your institution to provide your learners with an enhanced learning experience. Here are 6 benefits a competency-based learning model can provide your learners. Take a look and see how they can help your institution enhance your learning programs.

sections.heading: 1. Supports Personalised Learning
sections.content: In competency-based education, the learners have the ability to pursue the course at their own pace. They are able to concentrate on areas where they need improvement and skip areas they have mastered. This enables your institution to provide a personalised learning experience to your learners. Using a Learning Management System can help your institution simplify this process. For a more personalised approach, your institution can also implement digital badges. Digital badges can easily be shared on social media and help learners track their progress and showcase their achievements.

sections.heading: 2. Increases Learner Engagement
sections.content: Competency-based assessment is reliant on learners demonstrating their proficiency in a particular skill. This creates an environment with a high level of learner engagement. This is an incredible benefit for both your learners and your institution, as a higher engagement leads to a more successful learning experience. As studies have shown, more than 92% of educators believe that learner engagement is the key driver of success2.

sections.heading: 3. Enhances Learning Experience
sections.content: One of the key defining factors of competency-based learning is how the learners are evaluated. By emphasising the mastery of skills and competencies, competency-based learning requires the educator to assess the learner thoroughly. This leads to the learner receiving instant feedback and evaluations, enabling them to understand their strengths and weaknesses. Your institution can further enhance competency-based learning programs by incorporating gamification. By giving out points or digital badges for each skill learnt, your institution can drive friendly competition among your learners.

sections.heading: 4. Provides Career-Focused Learning
sections.content: The greatest hallmark of success for an educational institution is its learners succeeding in the career of their choice. This is one of the reasons why competency-based learning is so important. It enables learners to learn skills and competencies that are necessary for specific careers. This will allow your learners to spend their resources on specific skills that will enable them to progress in the career of their choice. Therefore, by adopting competency-based learning, your institution can better prepare your learners for the future.

sections.heading: 5. Enables Flexible Learning Pathways
sections.content: In traditional education, your learners will have to complete an entire course to gain competency in a subject. However, with competency-based learning, your institution can allow learners to pick and choose the skills and knowledge they want. This can be further supported by introducing micro-credentials. Micro-credentials will enable your learners to gain certifications for each skill or competency they have mastered. As opposed to gaining a single certificate for the completion of a course, this is more efficient.

sections.heading: 6. Fosters Inclusivity And Accessibility
sections.content: Education is a resource-intensive venture. From time to effort and money, education has many barriers. However, with competency-based education, learners can opt to spend their resources on learning only the skills they need. This will enable more learners to pursue education, making the learning experience more accessible. By adopting competency-based learning and assessment, your institution can take a valuable step towards a more inclusive environment. Competency-based learning and assessment is an incredible tool to enhance your learners. By enabling them to focus on the skills they need and learn at their own pace, it can personalise the learning experience. And by being flexible and accessible, it can foster a sense of inclusivity. Overall, competency-based learning is very beneficial to your learners, and it is here to stay. As the job market becomes increasingly competitive, adopting competency-based learning can help your institution prepare your learners for the future.

sections.heading: Competency-Based Learning With Digital Badges
sections.content: Competency-based learning in education is a field with incredible growth. And with growth comes innovative solutions. One of these innovative solutions is the use of digital badges to complement competency-based learning. Digital badges will help your institution enhance its competency-based learning programs in many ways. For example, it can help your institution implement micro-credentialing, helping your learners receive certifications for each skill they learn. It can also help your institution incorporate gamification concepts like a reward system. There are many more ways your institution can benefit by incorporating digital badges. As the world moves on to the future, using innovative ways to enhance your learning programs will benefit not just your institution but also your learners. Need more ways to enhance your learners? Book a demo with CredSure to learn how digital credentials can help your institution.

Increasing website traffic is a necessity for any business or organisation in the current world. This is true even for educational institutions as the world moves towards online learning. With the rise of online learning, which grew by 900% in the last two decades1, competition in the educational sector is at an all-time high. How can your institution stay on top when the competition is ever-growing? The answer is closer than you might expect. For educational institutions, the biggest source of branding is none other than its students. With social media and other avenues available for showcasing achievements, the digital certificates you issue can become one of the biggest tools available to drive website traffic. How? Take a look below.

sections.heading: 1. They Improve Brand Visibility
sections.content: Your digital certificates carry your brand name. Whenever someone sees a digital certificate you issue, they see the name of your institution and get a general idea of what you offer to your learners. So, they become more likely to visit your website if they are looking for a similar learning program.

sections.heading: 2. They Are Shareable
sections.content: Digital certificates are easily shareable across the internet. When your learners receive their certificates, they usually share them online through many platforms, including social media. This is a free promotion for your institution. Knowing that digital certificates can drive traffic to your institution’s website is only half the battle. Here are a few strategies on how to use them to increase traffic to your website.

sections.heading: 1. Encourage Social Sharing
sections.content: Giving your learners the opportunity to share their digital certificates is just the first step to driving website traffic. To maximise the chances of the digital certificates you issue being shared online, follow these tips: Tell your learners about the benefits of sharing digital certificates Use pop-ups and prompts on your website or LMS (Learning Management System) to encourage social sharing If your LMS uses a point system, give out points for each digital certificate shared online Ensure that one-click sharing for different social media platforms is available The goal here is to convince your learners that sharing their digital certificates on social media is beneficial to them. Once they are shared on social media, your digital certificates should have the ability to attract visitors to your website. That’s where the next tip becomes useful.

sections.heading: 2. Make Your Digital Certificates Visually Appealing
sections.content: Ensuring that the digital certificates issued by your institution are visually appealing has many benefits. One, they represent your institution, hence they are a reflection of your brand. The more visually appealing they are, the higher the chances of others finding your brand attractive. The other benefit of ensuring that the digital certificates issued by your institution are visually appealing is that it will encourage your learners to share them on social media. Ensuring that the colour palettes, fonts, and any imagery used go well with popular social media platforms is necessary to maximise the visual appeal of your digital certificates.

sections.heading: 3. Link To Your Website And Learning Programs
sections.content: The ultimate goal of encouraging your learners to share their digital certificates is to increase website traffic to your learning programs. To maximise the traffic to your website, follow these tips: Add a default text to the “share online” option with a link to your website. This will show up under the digital certificate when it is shared on social media On the public verifier page of the digital certificate, provide a link to your website in an easily visible area Provide links to your learning programs on the public verifier page If there is no catalogue of the learning programs provided by your institution, create one and add a link on the public verifier page If possible, host the digital certificate on the same domain as your website, ensuring that easy navigation to your website is possible

sections.heading: 4. Provide Links To Your Institution’s Social Media Profiles
sections.content: Just as important as it is to drive traffic to your institution’s website, it is necessary to do the same to its social media profiles. Social media is a great tool to provide information in a fun and engaging way to curious visitors. It is also an effective tool to drive traffic to your website through engaging social media posts. By providing links to your institution’s social media profiles, you can increase engagement with your audience and drive traffic to your website.

sections.heading: CredSure: Redefining The Future Of Digital Credentials
sections.content: Digital certificates are a great tool to market your institution. They are a visual representation of the benefits it can provide to your learners. They are also a great promotional material that can attract new learners to your learning programs. As the digital age transforms and the competition on the internet increases at an incredible rate, any tool that can increase website traffic is a boon. As one of the leading platforms for issuing digital certificates, CredSure recognises the impact of digital certificates on website traffic. The digital certificates issued by CredSure are visually appealing and can be shared on social media with a single click. The public verifier page of the certificates issued with the help of CredSure is completely customisable. Your institution can use personalised branding, add a detailed description of the course and include links to your website and learning programs. CredSure can help your institution enhance its learning programs with blockchain-anchored tamper-proof digital certificates that can also drive website traffic.
